Say if if the aficionado lifting the bells will spend three hundred dollars a year Those guys will spend three thousand dollars a year or more the price point of the product goes up because there's a money payoff to it So 10% of your customer base in the second business Could do the same volume that a hundred percent of your customer base is doing in the first business And No, no, I'm talking about John now being the guru of a business development and marketing see remember We had this conversation at the table With somebody else One of the things to remember about being a guru is is that your customers? Regardless of your point of entry to them or your presented area of specialty If they really connect with you they now want you to be a guru of many other things Because they ain't interested in going to find in seven dads They found you they like you they're happy with you. They want one dad So, you know, they may buy my time management stuff But what they'd really want is John's time management stuff How does John manage his time and get everything done and run five businesses and yada yada yada And and and and how does John what's what are John's? Advertising and marketing secrets that can be applied to any business etc. etc. etc And so they'll fuzzy the edges for you on their own with very little encouragement And so it a how long is a two-day deal or a three-day deal your kettlebell do Hickey So it's a three-day deal and out of that he's given him a two-hour little bonus marketing thing and they're all lathered up and Excited about that. Well, what does that tell you? It tells you for example, and if you want to try a quick and dirty experiment The next one you do in the two hours Sell a two-day marketing thing for 1500 bucks a piece at the back of the room and see how many sign up I'm gonna bet you That most of them march back to the room and sign up so See, but they'll always fuzzy the edges on you so they want you as soon as they've like really bought into you Say there. I mean my joke is all the time, you know and coaching calls and coaching meetings and everything You know, I get all kinds of questions about how to make the marriage work And I go, you know, I'm the last guy. I don't know nothing about that You know, I know nothing, but they want you to know it And so they project that on to you like we project human characteristics under our pets You know or me under the horses So, you know, I think I can talk to them and you know because you want that to be and you think they're understanding you You know and they're really just not in their head hoping you got a carrot But but but I mean we think they're getting it, you know, well your customers project on to you God-like status that extends beyond your area of specialty and in this case the more valuable customer Say so first of all, so he's got customers who are already in business They're already in sales as well as they want to be they want to be like John and it's easy to encourage that Right, they're probably better business customers than say ours Because the first thing they're doing involves discipline It's the martial arts philosophy. I mean by far our best customers are martial arts guys I'd rather have them than anything else So you're starting with a good one, but it's the dollars that they'll pay per product Again if you compare your product prices to our product prices compare your seminar prices to our seminar prices So the dollars they'll pay are much greater because there's a money pay off to that payment on the health and fitness side. So to whatever degree you would enjoy doing that business you already have Everything in place to do it and it might very well like overnight Be equivalent in profitability to the current business This is the first time I've ever seen John stuff was yesterday, but One is I mean when I first saw this my inclination was if I got this in the mail I would sort of scan scan it quickly and sort of try to see like I do a magazine and try to sort of see You know, which ones I might be interested in spend time with that So questions are I have three questions. I'll give them to you at one time one is is there any strategies That you've seen that encourages people to actually read this stuff more cover to cover similar to like what Will the grand strategy of paying them 50 bucks to listen to the CD strategy number two question number two is which may be linked to this is You know lately I've been looking at these Napa association things which you know has actual like content articles in there This thing when I looked at it's a straight catalog Somebody's just trying to sell me something. Is that a smart thing to add to this is content I Hold the third one or I'll get lost Can you give me the first two Well first of all the catalog business itself I don't claim to be the world's expert, but I have studied it and the catalog Thing the first thing that's pretty much universally true about catalogs is there a shitty way to get a new customer You'll almost always beat them with a solo direct mail campaign on one or two products that are your hot items They're not good for customer acquisition. They're very good for selling stuff to people with whom you already have a relationship and that helps the will they read it all because It yeah, so Like if I get a sharper image catalog, I'm a sharper image customer I buy from sharper image a couple times a year I will tend to go page by page by page Actually in search of anything that might interest me if I get a catalog from somebody. I don't have a relationship with I'm gonna do What you just did Kind of hop around zoop-zop, you know, so there's a certain amount of Goodwill that occurs because of the relationship having said that the editorial content issue is Dicey if you do too much of it you do it too well you actually wind up confusing people They don't know they got a catalog They don't know they're supposed to buy shit from it and they wind up with a great magazine, but you like Didn't make it clear to them. They were supposed to send you money, but a little of it done well Can help a lot Now not to be confused with the Natma magazine the magazine Which is really a magazine with catalog elements in it and so think of Weeder or think of Bill Phillips magazine or Life extension foundation where the article does lead to a product the article does lead to a product I think that's a useful vehicle, but I wouldn't do it in place of a true catalog the the best thing to do To get people to go through an entire catalog And it's also the best it coincidentally is the best thing to do to build the average order price Is tiered gifts based on size of order? Because what happens then is I try and find that one extra thing to buy to bump myself over the benchmark
